Full job description
Head of Analytics

A fast‑growing organisation is seeking a senior analytics leader to shape and advance its data strategy. This role will drive decision‑making across multiple business functions and ensure analytics becomes a core enabler of commercial and operational performance. You will also lead and develop a small team of analysts whose work will drive improvements in pricing, margin optimisation, product performance, operational efficiency and market understanding.

Key Responsibilities

Develop a clear analytics strategy aligned to organisational objectives.
Build and optimise reporting frameworks that support strategic planning and business performance.
Lead a team delivering insights into commercial trends, operational efficiency, performance indicators, and customer behaviour.
Partner with senior leaders to embed data‑driven thinking across departments.
Oversee integration of diverse data sources and ensure insight quality through robust governance and validation.
Create forecasting, trend analysis and scenario‑modelling approaches to support strategic decision‑making.
About You

10+ years' experience in analytics, insight, or business intelligence roles, including leading an analytics function.
Strong experience translating complex data into business‑focused recommendations.
Skilled in modern analytics tools (e.g., SQL, BI platforms, statistical/forecasting methods).
Proven capability developing high‑performing teams.
Comfortable working in environments undergoing transformation or modernisation.
Strategic, commercially aware, and able to engage effectively with senior stakeholders.
Why This Role?

Significant influence over organisation‑wide decision‑making.
Opportunity to shape a developing analytics capability.
Senior leadership exposure and contribution to long‑term strategic direction.
Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: £80,000.00-£120,000.00 per year

Benefits:

Canteen
Free flu jabs
Free parking
On-site parking
Work from home
Work Location: Hybrid remote in West Midlands Region
shortCompanyReason: Fast-growing organisation seeking a senior analytics leader.
shortRoleReason: Lead analytics strategy, team, and data-driven decision-making across the business.
companySummary: A fast-growing organisation with a developing analytics capability and a need for stronger data-led performance.
roleSummary: Senior analytics leadership role responsible for strategy, reporting, team management, governance, and insight delivery across commercial and operational areas.
headlineAttraction: Shape the organisation’s analytics strategy and influence decision-making at senior level.
rolePurpose: To lead analytics capability that enables commercial and operational performance through insight, reporting, and strategic support.
advertSummary: The organisation is hiring a Head of Analytics to shape data strategy, lead a small team, and improve decision-making through reporting, forecasting, and insight. The role focuses on commercial performance, operational efficiency, and senior stakeholder partnership in a hybrid West Midlands setting.
personalisedIntro: I bring a mix of operational control, reporting discipline and senior stakeholder support that fits a role like this well. My background is not pure analytics, but I have built and used performance reporting in complex environments where the quality of the insight mattered as much as the numbers themselves.
whyThisRole: This role fits the kind of work I do best: bringing structure to messy information, turning it into something leaders can use, and making sure it changes behaviour. I’d be comfortable with the mix of strategy, reporting, governance and team leadership, especially where the aim is to improve commercial and operational performance rather than just produce more dashboards.
companyPridePoints: Opportunity to shape a developing analytics capability
coreResponsibilities: Develop analytics strategy aligned to organisational objectives
Lead a team delivering business insights
Partner with senior leaders across departments
Oversee data integration, governance, and validation
Develop forecasting, trend analysis, and scenario modelling
essentialRequirements: 10+ years' experience in analytics, insight, or business intelligence
Ability to translate complex data into business recommendations
Experience with SQL and BI platforms
Experience with statistical or forecasting methods
Comfortable in transformation or modernisation environments
Able to engage with senior stakeholders
impliedStrategicGoals: Embed analytics as a core business enabler

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operational leader who turns data into decisions, control and measurable performance.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I bring a mix of operational control, reporting discipline and senior stakeholder support that fits a role like this well. My background is not pure analytics, but I have built and used performance reporting in complex environments where the quality of the insight mattered as much as the numbers themselves.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: I’m drawn to a fast-growing organisation because that usually means the analytics function has real room to shape how decisions get made. The brief suggests a business that wants stronger data-led performance, and I’d value the chance to help build that capability in a way that is practical, trusted and useful to senior leaders.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: This role fits the kind of work I do best: bringing structure to messy information, turning it into something leaders can use, and making sure it changes behaviour. I’d be comfortable with the mix of strategy, reporting, governance and team leadership, especially where the aim is to improve commercial and operational performance rather than just produce more dashboards.

selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I used reporting and operational control to support a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation, release £33.3k of working capital and improve cash at bank by £172.6k year on year. That shows I can connect data, control and business outcomes.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest proof point for commercial impact and decision support.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve used reporting and control to improve profit, cash and working capital, not just track them.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: This is the closest match to the reporting side of the role. I designed a way to see room usage, clinician status, waiting times, pathology pressure and prescription backlog in one place, which is the kind of visibility leaders need when they want faster decisions.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting example for reporting design, data integration and operational visibility.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve built reporting logic that helps leaders see pressure points quickly and act on them.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Cross-site continuous improvement across European theme parks
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: senior stakeholder partnership and performance improvement across functions
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led and supported improvement work across multiple major sites, using operational data and frontline insight to strengthen execution standards. That gives me relevant experience of working across different teams and turning insight into practical change.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as evidence of cross-functional influence and improvement in a complex environment.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’m used to using data and frontline insight to improve how different sites and teams perform.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I think this role needs someone who can give analytics a clearer shape, make reporting more useful, and earn trust with senior leaders. It also needs someone who can lead a small team, keep standards high on governance and validation, and make insight feel commercially relevant rather than technical for its own sake.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: analytics strategy aligned to business goals
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in roles where better control, reporting and financial discipline were tied directly to business performance, not treated as separate activities.
experienceMappings.relevance: That suggests I can help shape analytics around what the business actually needs to improve.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I built a live dashboard concept to bring fragmented operational data into one view so leaders could see pressure points quickly.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is directly relevant to building reporting that supports planning and decision-making.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: lead and develop a small analytics team
experienceMappings.myEvidence: At Experian I managed workload allocation and training across a 12-person function while maintaining service standards.
experienceMappings.relevance: That shows I can organise people, set expectations and keep delivery steady.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: translate complex data into business recommendations
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve used performance information to support improvements in profit, debtor control and cash, which means I’m comfortable turning data into action.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role needs insight that changes decisions, not just analysis that explains what happened.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: forecasting, trend analysis and scenario modelling
experienceMappings.myEvidence: My CV shows strong reporting and operational control, but it does not give a detailed forecasting case I can point to here.
experienceMappings.relevance: I would want to build on the forecasting side carefully and be honest about where my strongest evidence sits today.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked with fragmented operational data and built logic to make it usable in one place, which depends on clean definitions and reliable inputs.
experienceMappings.relevance: That matters in a role where insight quality and trust in the numbers are central.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked across multiple sites and functions, using operational data and frontline insight to support improvement with different stakeholders.
experienceMappings.relevance: This role needs someone who can work credibly with senior leaders across the business.

focusAreasToBring.title: Reporting that leaders trust
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on making reporting clearer, more consistent and easier to use, so leaders can see what is changing and where to act.

focusAreasToBring.title: Insight with commercial value
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep the work tied to pricing, margin, performance and efficiency, so analytics stays close to business priorities.

focusAreasToBring.title: Team discipline and standards
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would bring structure to the team’s workload, expectations and output quality, with a strong focus on useful delivery.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I’m a strong fit where analytics needs to be more than a technical function and has to influence how the business runs. My background combines reporting, operational control, team leadership and improvement work in environments where performance had to be visible and acted on. I would bring a practical style, commercial awareness and enough seniority to work well with leaders across the business.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend my first months tightening the shape of reporting, understanding the current data landscape and building trust with the leaders who rely on it. I’d also look to support the team so they can produce clearer insight on commercial trends, operational efficiency and performance drivers. Over time, I’d want to help make analytics more central to planning and decision-making.
companyHighlights: Hybrid working in the West Midlands Region
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where there is room to improve things properly and where leaders want clear, usable answers. The brief suggests a business that values practical data-led thinking, which suits the way I work.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the existing reporting, data sources, team workload and stakeholder expectations so I can see where the biggest gaps and quick wins are.
I would start tightening reporting definitions, checking data quality and working with senior stakeholders to make sure the insight being produced matches the decisions they need to make.

I would look to agree the main analytics priorities, support the team’s delivery rhythm and begin building more consistent insight around the commercial and operational areas the business cares about most.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring a steady, practical approach to analytics leadership and focus on making the function more useful to the business from day one.
closingProofPoints: I’ve supported a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation.
I built reporting across workflow, room utilisation, payroll and contractual income.
I’ve led improvement work across complex, multi-site and regulated environments.
contentNotes: I have kept the evidence grounded in operations, reporting and performance improvement because the CV does not show a pure analytics career history.
I have not claimed deep SQL or statistical modelling experience because that is not evidenced in the CV or shortlist.
I have kept forecasting references cautious and forward-looking because the available evidence is thin on that specific point.

examples.title: “Cross-site continuous improvement across European theme parks”
“Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
